strengths: Clear identification of three specific, real-world objects with strong contextual grounding (tennis racket, coffee photograph, magnesium supplement).
Explicit and sustained links to IA prompt 20, using Kolbâs experiential learning cycle and analysis of cognitive biases to frame knowledge claims.
Well-supported justifications for each object, with direct references to personal experience and external sources (including a works-cited list).
Insightful recognition of how physiological and psychological factors (caffeine effects, placebo responses) shape experiential knowledge.
weaknesses: Some repetitive phrasing (e.g., âwith experiences comes knowledgeâ) limits analytical precision and novelty.
Evidence base is uneven: relies on popular-science sources without consistent integration of peer-reviewed research.
Inconsistent citation formatting and missing access dates reduce scholarly rigor.
Image captions and contextual details are occasionally generic or placeholder, detracting from full real-world anchoring.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition consistently meets the 7â8 band descriptors: it clearly identifies three specific objects, situates them in real-world contexts, and provides strong, evidence-based justifications tied to IA prompt 20. The use of theoretical frameworks (Kolbâs cycle, cognitive biases) and a works-cited list demonstrate sustained academic engagement. Minor lapses in precision, occasional repetition, and formatting inconsistencies prevent it from reaching the 9â10 band, but the depth and clarity of analysis justify an upper-band score of 8.

annotations.comment: Object 3: âmy magnesium supplementâ is clearly named, establishing a focused real-world item for analysis.

The image provided appears more generic than personally contextualized. Consider including a photo showing the racket in use during a specific match to reinforce its real-world context and specificity.
The section header â(Commentary 1)â is generic. Rename it to specify âObject 1: Tennis Racketâ to improve clarity and guide the reader through your exhibition structure.
The student concretely situates the tennis racket in a specific tournament scenario, effectively establishing a clear real-world context for Object 1.
The integration of Kolbâs experiential learning cycle demonstrates a strong theoretical link to the IA prompt, illustrating how personal experimentation informs knowledge acquisition.
The phrase âif you are having a bad experience...â is repetitive and overly general. Focus on precise articulation of how reflection transforms specific experiences into actionable knowledge.
The sentence about âresponsibilityâ¦sub par experiencesâ is unclear. Rephrase to specify what responsibility you mean and how it links to knowledge development.
The assertion that experience and knowledge are interdependent is stated but not explicitly linked to evidence or prompt 20. Provide specific examples of how one shapes the other.
The student acknowledges emotional limitations in high-pressure situations, which strengthens the analysis by recognizing potential distortions in experiential knowledge.
There is no explicit mention of IA prompt 20 in this paragraph. Integrate the exact prompt wording to clarify alignment with the chosen question.
The placeholder â(Image)â is not descriptive. Include a caption explaining what the image represents and its real-world context for Object 2.
The photographic image of coffee lacks personal context (time, place, emotional reaction). Add details or a caption to ground it in your lived experience.
The discussion of personal alertness and focus demonstrates effective critical reflection on the coffee experience and its knowledge outcomes.
The statement that âwith experiences comes knowledgeâ is repeated from earlier sections. Aim for more precise and novel insights in each objectâs analysis.
No in-text citation is provided for the Harvard source. Insert parenthetical references when discussing specific research findings to strengthen evidence support.
The placeholder â(Image)â should be replaced by a descriptive caption indicating when and where the coffee photo was taken to anchor it in reality.
The coffee image itself could be contextualized with location and emotional details. Consider adding a brief narrative caption.
The suggestion to use âother testimonials as well as scientifically backed evidenceâ shows active engagement with external sources to validate experiential claims.
To strengthen your analysis, integrate specific study details (e.g., sample size, methodology) rather than referring generally to âscientifically backed evidence.â
The concluding paragraph reiterates earlier points about interdependence rather than deepening the analysis. Consider drawing a novel synthesis for Object 3.
Use a consistent citation style (e.g., APA or MLA) for your Works Cited list to enhance professional presentation.
Ensure every reference listed has a corresponding in-text citation to clearly link external evidence to specific claims in the commentary.
The consideration of a placebo effect reflects an insightful recognition of cognitive biases influencing experiential knowledge.
To broaden your evidential base, include peer-reviewed journal articles alongside popular-science sources for stronger credibility.
Include an accessed date for your IB Mastery blog source to align formatting across all references.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
